text:
can you please explain the structure and what is going on with the repo. it is for a geotab vibe coding competition. i guess this is the problem that i am solving:
I am building a dynamic garbage truck route optimizer for a hackathon. The system works as follows: there are a fixed set of garbage bin collection stops across a city, each bin has a real-time fill level (0–100%) from IoT sensors, and a garbage truck needs to visit only the bins above a fill threshold (e.g. 70%+) in the most efficient order possible. The set of "active" stops changes daily based on fill levels, so the route must be re-optimized dynamically — not precomputed.

go over my codebase and understand my project. in particular, read the vision.md file. it's not fully accurate but close enough. what are some metrics that i would want to show for the prediction dashboard.

go over the code and understand the code structure as well as the product we are building. specifically look at the vision.md file to understand the direction, and since i am person 2, focus on how person 2 would go about implementing the vision. once you have the context, i will ask you some questions before we start building.
ok, sounds good. can you give me the agreement in brief in a .md file so that i can send it to person 1 so that when they are coding they don't get mixed up.

looks good, now that this is done, i guess we can move to the actual implementation bit. before we add code to our algorithm and start working on it, i want you to make a very simple front-end according to your understanding of the app in smartroute.js so that we can run it and see if my algorithms are working. the person 1 will just edit this smartroute.js later right?
ok, so to test this, you are saying that i fork it and then run it?

ok, now that i have done all the setup stuff, i can actually move on to my implementation as person 2. before i do this, i want you do to summarize whatever we did and whatever is important to know in the repo as well as about the problem into a neat CLAUDE.md file. I'm gonna spin another agent who's gonna read this file and help me do the implementation.
actually, can you change the testing, i have forked this into my github so now i can just pull the branch and it should work. i am deleting the test-algo file.
read CLAUDE.md under ignore-docs. it basically has all the context you need to have to help me do my implementation now, i am person 2. u can read other documents as and when required.

before we start building, i wanna actually brainstorm on what should be the correct algorithm and metrics for each task. this is to say that the clarke-wright savings algorithm might not be the best algorithm we can use. we also need to do some brainstorming on what metrics we should focus on exactly for the predictive dashboard and everything. i guess we can do it step by step, but what i have is a research buddy who can help me with this. however, he just needs context on what the problem is, where i stand in regards to my algorithms and metrics and my reasonings behind it. i need you to make a document that outlines this. put it inside ignore-docs. additionally, have each task that we are optimizing for as steps (eg: algorithm is step 1, prediction metrics are step 2, etc, etc) so that my buddy doesn't get overwhelmed and only goes step by step. sounds good?

read step1.md. before implementation, i want you to confirm the input data for me. the way i see getting the input data right now is this: i am given an existing route by the fleet manager which contains bin locations as zones. then, i have my bin data for these same zones. then, my algorithm runs on this route given by the fleet manager when i press optimize route and it optimizes it. the way that the routes are shown in my add in is that every route is shown in map view, and then i can click on that route and click optimize now to optimize it. i can also play around with the sliders. is this how this is supposed to work?
what is routeplanitem?
so if there are 4 saved routes, how is this loaded? forget how it's done currently, is it possible to load it as 4 separate routes. imagine showing each route as a card.

ok, i guess make the required architecture changes as required in smartjouse.js then, cause this will help me do the testing. so, can you implement step1 in this way then, i don't really care if it breaks the existing agreements with person 1, i will just sort it out with them later. right now, i want the best possible version of the product based on my vision of the backend. 
here is what the product should do: it should have a search button to search for the route. any number of routes can be added. there is also a time filter to filter for routes within that particular date and time. as each route is added, it is shown on the front end in map view with the route planned out. then, the user adjusts the 2 sliders as they deem fit and then click on optimize route. this gives an overlay of the optimized route in the same map. let's say for example there are 4 routes. so, the map has a front end with these routes and when you click on a particular route, you can accept or deny the optimization request. if accepted, it creates a new route with the optimized version. if discarded, it exits. additionally, when clicking on each route after running optimize routes, there are metrics on the right side that aid you in making your decision, particularly 4 metrics: hours saved, fuel saved, co2 reduced and stops skipped. 
refer to the image.png as that visually shows what it looks like, just note that the 2nd slider is not there in the image.
before impelmenting, just confirm if you're not confused. if not, used my product vision, image.png and step1.md to create this beautful solution. architect this solution with comprehensive checkpoints along the way so that you don't fall down creating a completely useless solution.
i extended my limit, keep building
can u write an explanation for the main algorithm we are using and save it in a file called algorithm.md stored inside docs. this will be useful for my presentation later.

I have created the smartroute-algo.js, smartroute.js and smartroute.html files. here's the issue: when optimizing the route, it is just finding the shortest distance in the 2d space. however, i need to find a shortest route between two points that are accessible by roads in the map. would this be complicated to do, do i need to connect some sort of map api. additionally, i don't know why, but when i set the slider to 100%, it skips 17 steps but it's saying that it is saving 0 hours. is there some sort of bug in the code with the calculation?

perfect!!! when loading the data from the routes inside the geotab addin, will it just show the shortest line between bins before optimizing or will it show the actual route that the geotab system has. it would be important to show the actual route even before the optimization so that the fleet manager can visually see the difference between the two routes.
for the original route, i don't want to draw my own line using osrm tho. geotab already has route lines drawn, i wanna use those. are those not queryable?
read step3.md and execute. additionally, figure out how these prediction metrics would be displayed in the ui and display them too.

+8.3%/day · every ~8d
Collect: Mon, Thu

i suspect that the issue is to do with the following:
Map Add-In Specific Considerations:
If it's a Map Add-In, ensure that different Add-Ins do not use the same variable name, as this can cause errors and prevent the Add-In from working. It's recommended to use your solution name as a prefix for all your variables (e.g., for "ABC Add-In," use "abc" as a prefix).
When a user leaves and returns to the page, the iframe content is cleared and scripts are reloaded. If necessary, the current Add-In state can be saved in Local Storage, IndexedDB, or elsewhere.
Geotab Drive Add-In Considerations:
For Add-Ins designed for Geotab Drive, ensure that all dependencies (CSS, images, JavaScript) are included on creation or explicitly linked. If dynamic loading is required, these requests will fail without a network connection.
To display an Add-In on the Geotab Drive app, the item in your configuration file must have a path that equals "DriveAppLink/" (including a trailing forward slash).

I have a two fold task for you. firstly, my teammate has added the ui in the main branch. i have the backend as well a basic frontend in the backend-alg branch so that i can view my backend changes. firstly, how am i supposed to connect this backend to that ui without pushing my code to the main branch? secondly, my addin code is not working right now in geotab for some reason, after we connect the backend we would have to fix that too. just planning approaches for now

why is there smartroute-algo.js inside both dist and public. can we change it to be in backend folder and change all reference to there. additionally, i need a couple of features added. when a route is displayed at the start, it should display the actual road route assuming that all bins will be collected. then, after clicking optimize route it gets optimized as is already being done. however, the original route should still be shown in dotted lines to see the change visually. additionally, once the route is optimized, you should be able to click at the route which opens an overlay from which you can accept or discard optimization. if accepted, it writes the new route to geotab. additionally, when the route is clicked, the metrics on the right side change to only display the metrics associated with that route.
sorry some assets added in main in addin folder, can u add them in this branch so that i won't get conflicts when merging the branch?

i am confused. why don't we do this:
we populate say 10 mock routes using a script. each route has at least 10 locations, each being associated with a bin. we populate these bins with data in the data folder. we add current as well as historical data. 
then, it should work as expected right ?

workflow for the predictive model:
the user comes into the addin and selects a route as normal. instead of optimizing for the current sensor data, the user can select an arbitrary data in the future, and click on optimize route which optimizes the route for that day for that route based on predictive data (also gives a confidence score). the user can then click a button to accpet the optimized route. this can be extended so that the user can select all routes and click on optimize week, and the algorithm optimizes each route for the next week, giving it a date and time and exporting that as a planned route (the assumption would be that garbage trucks run 9-5, so everything fits within that).
to execute this, you would need a next-week forecast toggle in the ui layer. once that toggle is turned on, the routes can be selected as normal. you also have a select-all button that selects all existing routes. additionally, u have a time input field and checkbox for optimize week so that the user can do both actions. beyond these changes, the flow remains the same where the user clicks on optimize now and approves/denies optimizations.
Only confusing thing would then be what to do with the optimize week, do we make a planned route with day and time or do we just kinda optimize the routes individually and save them as unplanned routes itself.
Mostly everything is inside addin, look at what im saying here and see if it makes sense/ can be done/ needs any changes? i really need you right now. don't dive into the deep research report and shit for now, just make a plan and get shit done.

Can you help me write this based on our latest codebase:
The Story: A brief description of the problem you solved and your "vibe coding" journey:
Here's the markdown file for the script for your aid:
**3-Minute Presentation**

---

## Before You Start

- SmartRoute open in MyGeotab (or GitHub Pages preview), page refreshed
- No routes loaded, threshold at 50%, week toggle on "This Week"

---

## The Hook (20 sec)

> "Garbage trucks run fixed routes — same bins, same day, every week. They stop at half-empty bins while missing overflowing ones. A Stockholm pilot cut collection stops by 80% by only emptying bins that actually needed it. SmartRoute is a Geotab Add-In built for waste fleets. What makes it unique: it works with real-time sensor data when you have it, and with driver feedback or predictions from collection history when you don't. No lock-in to one vendor. Skip the low-priority bins, optimize the rest, write back to Geotab. Less driving, less CO₂, same service."

---

## Load + Optimize (1 min)

**Action:** Type "Downtown" → select "Downtown West." Add "Midtown East."
> "SmartRoute calls Geotab's API directly — `Get.Route`, `Get.Zone`, `Get.DeviceStatusInfo` — no separate login. When a route loads, the map shows each bin colored by fill level. Red is full, green is empty."

**Action:** Click "Optimize This Week's Routes."

> "We run Clarke-Wright Savings Algorithm plus OR-Opt — all client-side. Bins below the threshold get skipped. Each skipped stop saves ~5 minutes of engine idle time on top of the driving distance saved."

**Point at the stats:**

> "X stops skipped. X km saved. X litres of fuel. X kg of CO₂. And Geotab Ace fires in the background — it reads the optimization result and generates a one-sentence fleet insight."

---

## Review + Accept (45 sec)

**Action:** Click "Review Routes" — overlay appears bottom-left on the map.

> "The review panel floats on the map so you can see the optimized route while you review it. It shows before/after stop count, savings metrics, and — because we call `DeviceStatusInfo` — it assigns the nearest real Geotab vehicle to this route's depot using a Haversine distance calculation."

**Action:** Click the arrow to cycle routes — map auto-zooms to each one.

**Action:** Accept a route.

> "Accepting writes back to Geotab: `api.call('Add', { typeName: 'Route' })` with the optimized stop sequence. The route chip turns green. The driver sees the updated route in their GO app immediately."

---

## Next Week Forecast (45 sec)

**Action:** Click "Next Week Forecast" toggle.

> "Switch to Next Week and the data source changes — amber badge, 'Predicted fill levels.' Click Optimize."

> "For each bin, we project fill to next Monday: `currentFill + fillRatePerDay × daysToNextMonday`. Fill rates come from a linear regression on 4 weeks of collection history stored in AddInData. The result goes into a separate forecast state — it never overwrites this week's accepted routes."

**Point at the review overlay:**

> "'Preview only — not saved.' This is a look-ahead tool. Scroll down and you see the day-of-week summary: which bins hit threshold Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday. Plan your capacity and driver shifts before the week starts."

---

## The Close (10 sec)

> "Works on day one with no sensors. Sensor data plugs straight in when you have it. Sensor-agnostic, Geotab-native, and everything writes back to your database."

---

## If Asked: Judge Questions

**Algorithm?** See "How the Algorithm Works" below.

**Fill level source?** `AddInData` — seeded for demo. In production: bin sensors, driver feedback, or inferred from collection history. Sensor-agnostic.

**Geotab writeback real?** Yes — `Add.Route` + `RoutePlanItems` + Zones. Works against a live Geotab database.

**Next Week forecast?** `projectedFill = currentFill + fillRatePerDay × daysToNextMonday`. Linear regression on collection logs. Stored in `forecastOptimizedMap` — separate from live results.

**Ace API?** We pass km saved + stops skipped to Ace and get a natural language fleet insight back. Falls back to a CO₂-to-trees equivalent if Ace is unavailable.

---

## How the Algorithm Works

**Files:** `backend/smartroute-algo.js` (source) · `addin/src/services/algorithm.ts` (wrapper) · `addin/dist/smartroute-algo.js` (built copy)

### Flow (from `runWithDistFn` in smartroute-algo.js)

1. **Split by threshold** — Bins with `fillLevel >= threshold` are mandatory. The rest are candidates.

2. **Phase 1-A: Clarke-Wright Savings** — For every pair of bins (i, j), compute:
   ```
   saving = depot→i + depot→j - i→j
   ```
   Sort pairs by saving descending. Greedily merge routes: take the highest-saving pair first. If both bins are endpoints of different routes and the merged route doesn't exceed capacity (default 10 bins), merge them. Repeat until no more merges. Each route is a depot round-trip.

3. **Phase 1-B: OR-Opt** — For each route, try moving segments of 1, 2, or 3 consecutive bins to every other position. Accept the move only if it strictly reduces total route distance. Repeat until no improvement.

4. **Phase 2: Selective insertion** — For sub-threshold candidates:
   ```
   netValue = fillLevel - (alpha × normalizedInsertionCost)
   ```
   `alpha = intensity × 3` (maps the 0–1 slider to 0–3). Sort candidates by fillLevel descending. Insert candidates with `netValue > 0` at their cheapest insertion position. If `intensity = 0`, insert all candidates unconditionally at cheapest positions. High-fill bins get first dibs on cheap slots.

5. **Metrics** — `kmSaved` = original NN baseline vs optimized. `fuelSavedL = kmSaved × 0.3`. `co2AvoidedKg = fuelSavedL × 2.68`. `hoursSaved` includes driving time (25 km/h) + service time (5 min per bin). The wrapper in `algorithm.ts` adds `idleMinutesSaved = stopsSkipped × 5` for engine idle + collection time per skipped stop.

### Prediction model (from `predict` in smartroute-algo.js)

- Recency-weighted average of fill rates (exponential decay 0.8 — most recent logs weigh more)
- `fillRatePerDay` from consecutive collection log pairs: `fillPctAtCollection / daysBetween`
- `daysUntilThreshold = (threshold - currentFill) / fillRatePerDay`
- Confidence: high (≥5 observations, stdDev < 5), medium (≥3 or stdDev < 15), low otherwise
- Fleet-wide fallback: if a bin has < 2 observations, use the global fleet average fill rate (or 10%/day if no fleet data)

---

## Prize Alignment (tailor your pitch)

| Award | What to emphasize |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Vibe Master** | Innovation (threshold + forecast), utility (works today, no sensors), execution (Geotab-native, writes back) |
| **Innovator** | Clarke-Wright + OR-Opt + selective insertion; Geotab Ace for fleet insight; prediction model from collection history |
| **Disruptor** | Same pattern for waste, deliveries, inspections, service — not locked to one vertical |
| **Best Use of Google Tools** | Geotab Ace API for natural-language fleet insight; optional Google Distance Matrix for road distances |
| **Green Award** | CO₂ avoided, fuel saved, idle time eliminated; "less driving, same service" |

---

## Hook Sources (if asked)

- **80% stop reduction:** Stockholm pilot — 7,138 stops/week → 1,072 with sensor-based collection (Connected Bins / Kista)
- **Half-empty bins:** Traditional fixed routes "repeatedly visit half-empty bins while missing overflow situations" (Smart Ends, BrighterBins)
- **30% cost reduction:** Edinburgh Council, 11,000 bins with smart sensors (BrighterBins case study)
- **Sensor + human:** Most fleets still rely on driver observation; SmartRoute supports both sensor data and driver feedback / historical inference

# SmartRoute — Demo Script
**3-Minute Presentation**

---

## Before You Start

- SmartRoute open in MyGeotab (or GitHub Pages preview), page refreshed
- No routes loaded, threshold at 50%, week toggle on "This Week"

---

## The Hook (20 sec)

> "Garbage trucks run fixed routes — same bins, same day, every week. They stop at half-empty bins while missing overflowing ones. A Stockholm pilot cut collection stops by 80% by only emptying bins that actually needed it. SmartRoute is a Geotab Add-In built for waste fleets. What makes it unique: it works with real-time sensor data when you have it, and with driver feedback or predictions from collection history when you don't. No lock-in to one vendor. Skip the low-priority bins, optimize the rest, write back to Geotab. Less driving, less CO₂, same service."

---

## Load + Optimize (1 min)

**Action:** Type "Downtown" → select "Downtown West." Add "Midtown East."
> "SmartRoute calls Geotab's API directly — `Get.Route`, `Get.Zone`, `Get.DeviceStatusInfo` — no separate login. When a route loads, the map shows each bin colored by fill level. Red is full, green is empty."

**Action:** Click "Optimize This Week's Routes."

> "We run Clarke-Wright Savings Algorithm plus OR-Opt — all client-side. Bins below the threshold get skipped. Each skipped stop saves ~5 minutes of engine idle time on top of the driving distance saved."

**Point at the stats:**

> "X stops skipped. X km saved. X litres of fuel. X kg of CO₂. And Geotab Ace fires in the background — it reads the optimization result and generates a one-sentence fleet insight."

---

## Review + Accept (45 sec)

**Action:** Click "Review Routes" — overlay appears bottom-left on the map.

> "The review panel floats on the map so you can see the optimized route while you review it. It shows before/after stop count, savings metrics, and — because we call `DeviceStatusInfo` — it assigns the nearest real Geotab vehicle to this route's depot using a Haversine distance calculation."

**Action:** Click the arrow to cycle routes — map auto-zooms to each one.

**Action:** Accept a route.

> "Accepting writes back to Geotab: `api.call('Add', { typeName: 'Route' })` with the optimized stop sequence. The route chip turns green. The driver sees the updated route in their GO app immediately."

---

## Next Week Forecast (45 sec)

**Action:** Click "Next Week Forecast" toggle.

> "Switch to Next Week and the data source changes — amber badge, 'Predicted fill levels.' Click Optimize."

> "For each bin, we project fill to next Monday: `currentFill + fillRatePerDay × daysToNextMonday`. Fill rates come from a linear regression on 4 weeks of collection history stored in AddInData. The result goes into a separate forecast state — it never overwrites this week's accepted routes."

**Point at the review overlay:**

> "'Preview only — not saved.' This is a look-ahead tool. Scroll down and you see the day-of-week summary: which bins hit threshold Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday. Plan your capacity and driver shifts before the week starts."

---

## The Close (10 sec)

> "Works on day one with no sensors. Sensor data plugs straight in when you have it. Sensor-agnostic, Geotab-native, and everything writes back to your database."

---

**Geotab writeback real?** Yes — `Add.Route` + `RoutePlanItems` + Zones. Works against a live Geotab database.

**Next Week forecast?** `projectedFill = currentFill + fillRatePerDay × daysToNextMonday`. Linear regression on collection logs. Stored in `forecastOptimizedMap` — separate from live results.

**Ace API?** We pass km saved + stops skipped to Ace and get a natural language fleet insight back. Falls back to a CO₂-to-trees equivalent if Ace is unavailable.

---

## How the Algorithm Works

**Files:** `backend/smartroute-algo.js` (source) · `addin/src/services/algorithm.ts` (wrapper) · `addin/dist/smartroute-algo.js` (built copy)

### Flow (from `runWithDistFn` in smartroute-algo.js)

1. **Split by threshold** — Bins with `fillLevel >= threshold` are mandatory. The rest are candidates.

2. **Phase 1-A: Clarke-Wright Savings** — For every pair of bins (i, j), compute:
   ```
   saving = depot→i + depot→j - i→j
   ```
   Sort pairs by saving descending. Greedily merge routes: take the highest-saving pair first. If both bins are endpoints of different routes and the merged route doesn't exceed capacity (default 10 bins), merge them. Repeat until no more merges. Each route is a depot round-trip.

3. **Phase 1-B: OR-Opt** — For each route, try moving segments of 1, 2, or 3 consecutive bins to every other position. Accept the move only if it strictly reduces total route distance. Repeat until no improvement.

4. **Phase 2: Selective insertion** — For sub-threshold candidates:
   ```
   netValue = fillLevel - (alpha × normalizedInsertionCost)
   ```
   `alpha = intensity × 3` (maps the 0–1 slider to 0–3). Sort candidates by fillLevel descending. Insert candidates with `netValue > 0` at their cheapest insertion position. If `intensity = 0`, insert all candidates unconditionally at cheapest positions. High-fill bins get first dibs on cheap slots.

5. **Metrics** — `kmSaved` = original NN baseline vs optimized. `fuelSavedL = kmSaved × 0.3`. `co2AvoidedKg = fuelSavedL × 2.68`. `hoursSaved` includes driving time (25 km/h) + service time (5 min per bin). The wrapper in `algorithm.ts` adds `idleMinutesSaved = stopsSkipped × 5` for engine idle + collection time per skipped stop.

- `fillRatePerDay` from consecutive collection log pairs: `fillPctAtCollection / daysBetween`
- `daysUntilThreshold = (threshold - currentFill) / fillRatePerDay`

- Fleet-wide fallback: if a bin has < 2 observations, use the global fleet average fill rate (or 10%/day if no fleet data)

| Award | What to emphasize |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Vibe Master** | Innovation (threshold + forecast), utility (works today, no sensors), execution (Geotab-native, writes back) |
| **Innovator** | Clarke-Wright + OR-Opt + selective insertion; Geotab Ace for fleet insight; prediction model from collection history |

Geotab Vibe Coding Competition
​🎦 Replay: Winning the Hackathon with Google Cloud

​In this live session, host Felipe Hoffa (Data+AI Advocate, Geotab) will answer your questions while Mahin Sheth (Customer Engineer, Data & AI, Google) shows off the best Google Cloud vibe coding tools (Gemini Canvas, AI Studio, & Firebase).

​More food for thought recordings.
🚀 Competition Window: Feb 12 – March 2, 2026

​🚀 Geotab Vibe Coding Competition, Sponsored by Google Cloud:
​From Prompt to Pavement: Redefining Fleet Intelligence
​This is an open invitation for you to reimagine the world of transportation. We are moving away from rigid requirements toward a creative "Zero to Hero" roadmap. We provide the data and the AI connector; you provide the vision.

​Prompt Your Vision into Reality
​We’ve bridged Geotab’s data with the world’s most powerful AI models.

​The Method: Describe → Generate → Refine.

​The Goal: Functional prototypes that solve real problems.

​The Playground: Real-world simulated data (GPS, diagnostics, fuel, safety events).

​📅 Timeline
​Competition (participation): Feb 12 – Mar 2, 2026

​Submission Deadline: March 2, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST

​Judging: Mar 3 - 22, 2026

​Winner Announcement: Mar 23, 2026 

​What Can You Build? (Inspiration)
​We expect to see a wide range of solutions. Here are a few paths you can take:

​Custom Add-Ins (No-Code): Use the Google Gem Architect to prompt a new MyGeotab page into existence in minutes.

​Custom Add-Ins (GitHub): Build a more complex UI hosted on GitHub and integrate it directly into the MyGeotab platform.

​Stand-Alone Platforms: Create a separate website or unique dashboard that visualizes fleet data for a specific audience.

​Enterprise Integration: Sync Geotab data with tools like SAP or Salesforce to automate business workflows.

​Conversational Intelligence: Build new MCP Skills that allow managers to "chat" with their fleet to get instant insights.

​Fleet Gamification: Build a game—create leaderboards for driver safety or fuel-efficiency challenges.

​Niche Solutions: Solve a specific pain point for industries like Construction, Waste Management, or Logistics.


User Uploaded Image
​🏆 Prize Pool ($25,000 Total)
​The Vibe Master ($10,000): Best overall solution- Innovation, utility, and execution.

​The Innovator ($5,000): Exceptional technical creativity and use of AI.

​The Disruptor ($2,500): Most unique or surprising "outside-the-box" idea.

​Best Use of Google Tools  ($2,500): Best application of AI reasoning using Google Tools.

​Green Award ($2,500): Best solution for sustainability or EV optimization.

​Most Collaborative ($2,500): The Community Hero: Most active in helping others on Reddit/GitHub.*

​*"Most Collaborative" is an individual award given to one person, stackable with other awards.

​

​🔮 Your Toolkit: The Vibe Coding Stack
​We’ve curated a set of tools to help you move from an idea to a working prototype in record time. Whether you are a seasoned developer or a first-time "vibe coder," these resources are your foundation.

​The Single Source of Truth

​The Geotab Vibe Guide (GitHub): This is your mission control. It contains the 60-minute "Zero to Hero" roadmap, sample prompts, guides, skills, and tools you’ll need to get started. ☝️Bookmark this first.

​What You’ll Use

​Geotab Demo Account (Required): We provide the fuel. You will get access to a free MyGeotab Simulator account packed with live-simulated data—trips, engine diagnostics, fuel levels, and safety events—so you can build without needing physical hardware.

​AI Assistants (Your Co-Pilot): This is vibe coding. You are free (and encouraged) to use any AI-assisted coding tools you prefer—Gemini, Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot. Use them to generate boilerplate, debug logic, and translate your natural language prompts into working software.

​Google Cloud & Gemini (Encouraged): While the invitation is tool-agnostic, we highly encourage the use of Google Gemini 3.0 and Google Cloud. Solutions that leverage the speed of Gemini Flash or the deep reasoning of Gemini Pro for fleet insights are top contenders for the Intelligence Award.

​The Support Hub

​r/geotab (Reddit): This is our dedicated Reddit thread to discuss the Hackathon! We will use it for:

​Technical AMAs with Geotab engineers (Felipe and Aaron).

​Teammate matching and brainstorming.

​Troubleshooting your "vibe" when the code isn't cooperating.

​🌟 Note: Activity here counts toward the Most Collaborative prize!

​Submission Requirements (Due Mar 2, 2026)
​The Demo: A 3-minute video showing your solution in action.

​The Code: A link to your public GitHub repository (including the prompts used).

​The Story: A brief description of the problem you solved and your "vibe coding" journey.

​👉 Submit here

​The Fine Print (T&C Highlights)
​Ownership: You own your code. Geotab has a non-exclusive license to showcase your project.

​Eligibility: 18+; global entry (standard exclusions apply). Void in Quebec, Brazil, and Italy, and Geotab Restricted Countries. (check the Terms & Conditions for more details).

​

​Note: Aiming for the prizes? Please read the Terms and Conditions below to make sure your entry qualifies for prizes. Otherwise, this is a fun learning experience open for everyone to enjoy!

​By registering, you agree to the Terms & Conditions and consent to the processing of your data as outlined in Geotab’s Privacy Policy.
